report_name: Pro-Iranian Hacktivists "Handala" Claim Attack on US Medical Tech Firm Stryker

threat_actors.name: Handala
threat_actors.description: A pro-Iranian hacktivist group that claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against Stryker. Their attacks are politically motivated, aiming to cause disruption, chaos, and serve as propaganda.
threat_actors.goals: To cause chaos, disruption, and serve as a tool for political messaging and intimidation against US and Israeli-aligned targets.

attack_patterns.name: Internal Defacement
attack_patterns.description: Used by hacktivist groups like Handala to modify the content of a public-facing website to display political messages.

attack_patterns.name: Network Denial of Service
attack_patterns.description: Used by hacktivist groups like Handala by flooding a website or service with traffic to make it unavailable to legitimate users.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used by hacktivist groups like Handala to gain initial access for defacement by exploiting well-known vulnerabilities like SQL injection or cross-site scripting in web applications.

identities.name: Stryker
identities.description: A US-based medical technology company targeted by the Handala hacktivist group.

indicators.name: Potential Web Application Attack Pattern
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Web Application Attack Pattern
id: 2b8c1f9d-5a3e-4b7c-8d1f-9a0b3c2d1e8f
status: experimental
description: Detects common SQL injection or Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) patterns in URL requests, which could indicate an attempt to exploit public-facing applications.
references:
  - https://www.570news.com/2026/03/29/hacked-hospitals-hidden-spyware-iran-conflict-shows-how-digital-fight-is-ingrained-in-warfare/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-03-29
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection_sqli:
    cs-uri-query|contains:
      - ' union '
      - ' select '
      - 'information_schema'
      - ' and 1=1'
      - ' or 1=1'
  selection_xss:
    cs-uri-query|contains:
      - '<script>'
      - 'alert('
      - 'onerror='
      - 'onload='
  condition: 1 of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate application traffic that uses similar keywords in parameters.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects common SQL injection or Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) patterns in URL requests, which could indicate an attempt to exploit public-facing applications as mentioned in the article.
